title: The Brew Shop - Weekly Email Update
contentHtml:
Hey Brew Shop Fam!
Here's an update of what's happening this week - from new beer drops, wine reviews, our current tap list, to upcoming tastings, and more. 

Join us this Friday from 5-7 PM as we welcome DC Brau for our free tasting! Scroll down to see our full tasting line-up for July.

🌳 Hazy fav, Little Willow
• Beachcomber DDH IPA with Citra & Sabro
• Growth Spurt DDH DIPA with Dolcita & Citra

🍃 Parish delivering the hops
• Saints and Sinners West Coast DIPA - collab with Saint Arnold
• Fresh drop of Ghost in the Machine DIPA with Citra
• Gulf Coast IPA returns

🍻 Straight from ATL, Halfway Crooks
• Var Czech Pilsner
• Ada German Pilsner
• Mavy Czech Dark Lager

🪴 Fonta Flora x 6!
• Lake James Rind lager with heritage corn, local watermelon, fresh lime & tajin
• Gator Jaw Lockdown English-style best bitter
• Lion’s Tooth West Coast IPA with dandelion flowers plus Citra, Kohatu, Perle & Rakau
• Aestival West Coast IPA with Strata, Mosaic & HBC 630
• Double Hop Beard West Coast DIPA with Citra, Mosaic & Simcoe
• Triple Hop Beard West Coast Triple IPA with Amarillo, Citra, Mosaic & Simcoe

❤️ The Veil dishing out hops
• Bone West Coast IPA with Chinook, Citra, Mosaic, Columbus, Mosaic LupoMax, Nelson Sauvin, Citra LupoMax & Columbus Cryo
• Hide Your Heart3 Hazy DIPA with Citra & Sabro
• Idonteverwanttobu 0 IBU Hazy DIPA with Citra & Mosaic
• Limb West Coast IPA with CTZ, Chinook, Amarillo, Simcoe & Mosaic

🎶 Good Word = Good Hops
• Vinyl Records of the Cosmos West Coast IPA
• Van Allen Belt Wins Again Hazy IPA with Motueka, Columbus, Rakau & Peacherine
• Regular Magic West Coast IPA with Simcoe, Mosaic, Cryo

🫰 Oh yeah, new Prairie!
• Oh Yeah! sour ale with Tropical Punch Kool-Aid
• Smoothie Esses sour ale with smoothie flavored candy
• Peanut Butter Bomb Imperial Stout aged on Peanut Butter Roasted Coffee, Chocolate, Vanilla Beans & Ancho Chile Peppers

🌴 Big flavors from Commonwealth
• Redwoods West Coast IPA with Chinook, Columbus & Citra
• Bahama Mama Gose with Coconut, Orange, Pineapple & Pomegranate

🐧 KCBC keeping it fresh
• Sun Serpent Golden Ale
• Penguins on the Pitch West Coast Pale Ale with Mosaic & Cascade

🦉 Feeling Outdoorsy
• Eredita Little Owl Helles Bock
• Little House Lawnmower Beer Pale Ale with orange zest

👏Happy anniversary, Crooked Run!
• Noriega Hazy Triple IPA with Citra & Galaxy
• Approaching Black: PB & Chocolate Porter
• Anniversary Pils

💕 Local love
• Denizens Macadocious Maibock
• Silver Branch Mondo Mundo Mexican-style Vienna Lager
• Solace Prstige Wrldwide Hazy IPA with Motueka & Taiheke

🪄 Grimm bringing the magic
• Eight of Wands West Coast IPA with Mosaic, Strata & Riwaka
• Magical Realism Hazy DIPA with Galaxy, Nelson Sauvin & Citra

🍺 Thirst quenchers
• Bingo Rheinhaus Kolsch
• Shiner Ruby Redbird

🌈 All rainbows with Benchtop
• Eroica Foeder Conditioned Vienna Lager (collab with Lost Generation)
• Equal Hazy IPA with Cashmere, Lemon Drop & orange zest

👄 Pucker Up!
• Vasen Pineapple Passion Otter Sour
• Tox Eel-ectric Peach Lemonade Sour

🤠 Austin’s Live Oak is back!
• Pilz
• Hefeweizen

👄 Fan favorites!
• Cigar City White Oak Jai Alai West Coast IPA on white oak spirals
• Troegs Sunshine Salt & Lime Pils

Vasen Third Drop Pils

Zero Gravity Green State Extra Mexican Lager

Precarious Brewery Boys Want to Believe West Coast DIPA

New wines in the door!

Marcel Pierre Brut Rose Champagne: Hailing from the prestigious terroir of Épernay in the very heart of Champagne country, Marcel Pierre crafts this elegant Brut Rosé. A bouquet of roses you can drink with bright raspberry and cherry, light floral whiffs and toasted almond.

Mokoroa Sauvignon Blanc: Mokoroa comes from the Awatere Valley, a cooler sub-zone of Marlborough, which lends the wine an extra snap of freshness and precision that sets it apart from your average New Zealand Sauvignon Blanc. Lively and refreshing with nectarine, pink citrus, passion fruit and green grass.

Gran Sasso Chardonnay: Gran Sasso takes its name from the Gran Sasso d'Italia — the "Great Stone of Italy" — the dramatic mountain massif that forms the centerpiece of the Parco Nazionale del Gran Sasso in Abruzzo, home to the highest peaks in continental Italy south of the Alps. Done in all stainless steel (no oak here!) for a fresh, Italian take on the classic grape with crisp green apple, tropical fruit and a dry finish.

Schlink Haus Spatlese Riesling: The Schlink family has been producing wines from Germany's Nahe region since 1885, bottling their wines for generations in an iconic cobalt blue bottle — and Schlink Haus holds the distinction of being the first commercial blue bottle imported into the USA. Essentially a tropical vacation in your glass with preserved peach, ripe mango and guava.

Ciacci Piccolomini d'Aragona Toscana: A classic "pizza wine" the Ciacci is eaaaaaasy drinking at its best. Fruity with hints of spice and earth, and a great punch of acidity. Aging in stainless steel and concrete keep this medium bodied wine smooth, bright, and zippy.

Domaine L'Arnesque Cotes du Rhone: A warm, generous Southern Rhône red — think ripe dark fruit, sun-baked earth, and a touch of wild garrigue — the kind of honest, food-friendly bottle that reminds you why Grenache rules the south of France (and The Brew Shop).

Torremilanos Montecastrillo Tinto: We all agreed that this bottle of Tempranillo is the definition of our "Rustic" wine section. Old world and old school with gentle blackberry fruit paired with notes of tobacco, leather and wonderfully aggressive tannins. Biodynamic.

 

Marcel Pierre Brut Rose Champagne: Hailing from the prestigious terroir of Épernay in the very heart of Champagne country, Marcel Pierre crafts this elegant Brut Rosé. A bouquet of roses you can drink with bright raspberry and cherry, light floral whiffs and toasted almond.

Mokoroa Sauvignon Blanc: Mokoroa comes from the Awatere Valley, a cooler sub-zone of Marlborough, which lends the wine an extra snap of freshness and precision that sets it apart from your average New Zealand Sauvignon Blanc. Lively and refreshing with nectarine, pink citrus, passion fruit and green grass.

Gran Sasso Chardonnay: Gran Sasso takes its name from the Gran Sasso d'Italia — the "Great Stone of Italy" — the dramatic mountain massif that forms the centerpiece of the Parco Nazionale del Gran Sasso in Abruzzo, home to the highest peaks in continental Italy south of the Alps. Done in all stainless steel (no oak here!) for a fresh, Italian take on the classic grape with crisp green apple, tropical fruit and a dry finish.

Schlink Haus Spatlese Riesling: The Schlink family has been producing wines from Germany's Nahe region since 1885, bottling their wines for generations in an iconic cobalt blue bottle — and Schlink Haus holds the distinction of being the first commercial blue bottle imported into the USA. Essentially a tropical vacation in your glass with preserved peach, ripe mango and guava.

Ciacci Piccolomini d'Aragona Toscana: A classic "pizza wine" the Ciacci is eaaaaaasy drinking at its best. Fruity with hints of spice and earth, and a great punch of acidity. Aging in stainless steel and concrete keep this medium bodied wine smooth, bright, and zippy.

Domaine L'Arnesque Cotes du Rhone: A warm, generous Southern Rhône red — think ripe dark fruit, sun-baked earth, and a touch of wild garrigue — the kind of honest, food-friendly bottle that reminds you why Grenache rules the south of France (and The Brew Shop).

Torremilanos Montecastrillo Tinto: We all agreed that this bottle of Tempranillo is the definition of our "Rustic" wine section. Old world and old school with gentle blackberry fruit paired with notes of tobacco, leather and wonderfully aggressive tannins. Biodynamic.

Welcome to the shop to The Diver Brut Rose! Dive into this soft, bubbly rosé that can pair wonderfully with any occasion, from a casual night in to a fancy brunch. Its summery aroma of red berries and honeysuckle is followed by the light tasting notes of strawberries, cherries, kiwis, and a hint of that sweet, warm-weather favorite, shortcake. This Brut comes to the shop from a mother-daughter duo who believe that great wine should be approachable and delicious. We couldn’t agree more, as this wine brings its Prosecco-like texture from the Charmat method, and its light, warm-toned red-orange color will remind us of those summer sunsets. As we venture into the summer season, fully sip by the pool or watch the sunset with this bubbly by your side.

This week’s writeup is brought to you by Sam B.

July Tasting Events coming your way!

Thanks for your business! See you soon, Beth, Julie & Aidan and the entire Brew Crew
